political.s: <cite index="81-2,81-3">L'Oréal SA contributed $95,996 to campaigns and spent $70,000 on lobbying in the 2024 cycle.</cite> <cite index="82-1">In 2025, L'Oréal SA spent $180,000 lobbying.</cite> The company maintains a bipartisan stance with minimal political donations, focusing primarily on lobbying efforts related to trade, tariffs, and regulatory matters.

charity.s: <cite index="34-1">L'Oréal's Fund for Women targets highly vulnerable women to help them get out of poverty and support women victims of gender-based violence.</cite> <cite index="26-24,26-26">The company provided assistance to 2.2 million women and girls within communities in 2024.</cite> <cite index="27-22,27-23">The Fund for Nature Regeneration has invested around $29 million into global projects, and L'Oréal has helped over 71,000 people from disadvantaged communities gain employment.</cite>
charity.focus: Women empowerment, poverty alleviation, environmental regeneration, employment access

environment.s: <cite index="30-2,30-5">L'Oréal achieved a triple A rating from CDP in 2022, placing it among only 12 companies with this distinction.</cite> <cite index="23-7">L'Oréal's European production sites achieved 100% renewable energy in early 2025.</cite> However, <cite index="27-9,27-10,27-11">the company fell short on packaging goals, with only 49% of plastic packaging meeting refillable/reusable/recyclable standards by 2025, and just 37% of packaging materials being recycled or biobased.</cite>

labor.s: <cite index="42-22,42-23">L'Oréal recognizes workers' right to adequate living standards and ensures employees are paid living wages calculated in line with best practices.</cite> <cite index="43-20,43-21">79% of employees report engagement in internal surveys, and L'Oréal remains an attractive employer with over 1.3 million applications received in 2023.</cite> <cite index="43-29,43-30">The company's apprenticeship program celebrates 30 years with a 83% increase in work-study contracts over a decade.</cite>

dei.s: <cite index="59-1">L'Oréal ranked third in the 2023 FTSE Diversity & Inclusion Index Top 100.</cite> <cite index="53-4">L'Oréal has teams of 168 nationalities accounting for all skin types, genders, identities, cultures, and ages.</cite> <cite index="55-8">The company has trained more than 65,000 employees on diversity, equity and inclusion since 2007.</cite>

animals.s: <cite index="11-1,11-2">L'Oréal states it does not test on animals and is cruelty-free.</cite> However, <cite index="12-2">PETA lists L'Oréal as testing on animals because it refuses a company-wide policy against tests for both ingredients and finished products, and sells cosmetics in China where animal testing by government agencies is required by law.</cite> <cite index="12-7,12-9">Some L'Oréal products sold in China are required by law to be tested on animals.</cite>

guns.s: L'Oréal is a beauty and cosmetics company with no involvement in firearms manufacturing, sales, or distribution.

privacy.s: <cite index="71-1,71-18">L'Oréal states it does not offer or sell personal data.</cite> <cite index="71-13,71-20,71-21">The company commits to embedding privacy and security in all operations and keeping data safe through trusted partners only.</cite> <cite index="71-46,71-47,71-48">L'Oréal transfers personal data outside the EEA only in secure and lawful ways, reviewing third-party standards and entering into appropriate contracts.</cite>

execPay.s: <cite index="66-1,66-8">L'Oréal CEO Nicolas Hieronimus received €2.3 million in fixed remuneration and €2.7 million in annual variable remuneration in 2025, with total compensation reaching €12 million including shares.</cite> <cite index="63-10">His compensation is above average for similar-sized companies in the French market.</cite> CEO pay has increased while company earnings declined.

fec.committeeName: MAXAR TECHNOLOGIES HOLDINGS INC. PAC/FKA SPACE SYSTEMS/LORAL INC PAC
